posts.title: Telematic Concert as a Technical Team Member.
posts.description: Unlimited Sense - Warmth of Non Contact
posts.excerpt: On January 23, I participated in a telematic concert (remote concert) by College of Music at the Seoul National University “Unlimited Sense - Warmth of Non Contact “as a technical team member. Technical team members tried their best to minimize latency (a time delay) and give technical support for musicians from California and South Korea. The concert was recorded and it is available on YouTube.
posts.content:
On January 23, I participated in a telematic concert (remote concert) by College of Music at the Seoul National University “Unlimited Sense - Warmth of Non Contact “as a technical team member. Technical team members tried their best to minimize latency (a time delay) and give technical support for musicians from California and South Korea. The concert was recorded and it is available on YouTube.

I appreciate Professor Mark Dresser for giving me this opportunity.

posts.title: I won an ICAM Research Award
posts.description: I received an ICAM Award for my project, picturesque instruments.
posts.content:
I’m honored to be an ICAM Research Award winner this year. I received this award for my project, picturesque instruments. Check out my presentation for the details.

I appreciate Professor Shahrokh Yadegari for being such an amazing advisor.

posts.title: Paper Accepted to CHI 2022
posts.content:
Our paper was accepted to 2022 ACM CHI Conference on Human Factors in Computing Systems and I am excited to present our work at the conference. The paper is about a virtual reality meditation game that leverages spatial audio.

I appreciate Professor Juhan Nam and Young Yim Doh for advising.

posts.title: Presented Our Work at CHI 2022
posts.description: Also, our papers were published on ACM Library.
posts.excerpt: Due to COVID-19, I participated in CHI 2022 remotely. Due to the time difference, I had to wake up very early in the morning (like 5:00 AM). I presented our work, the melody of the mysterious stones, as a main presenter. I appreciate all those meaningful questions and comments for our work. I would also like to show my appreciation to Jaeran for her amazing work and Professor Young Yim Doh for taking a screenshot of our presentation.
posts.content: Due to COVID-19, I participated in CHI 2022 remotely. Due to the time difference, I had to wake up very early in the morning (like 5:00 AM). I presented our work, the melody of the mysterious stones, as a main presenter. I appreciate all those meaningful questions and comments for our work. I would also like to show my appreciation to Jaeran for her amazing work and Professor Young Yim Doh for taking a screenshot of our presentation.

content_blocks.content_html:
Machine Learning is one of the most exciting things that I have ever learned. Among sub-fields of artificial intelligence, I would summarize my research interest as natural language processing for music. (I am one of the researchers who understand that language is the key that helps connection among music-lovers, and among musicians.) I am very lucky to be advised by Juhan Nam for my research at Music and Audio Computing Lab at KAIST. Before coming here, I gained my bachelor's degree in Interdisciplinary Computing and the Arts from the University of California San Diego where I graduated magna cum laude with ICAM Research Award. For my side-projects (mostly for classes), I have developed some VR applications that leverage spatial audio.

Besides research, I like teaching. I have taught

  • Mathematics for Machine Learning and Natural Language Processing on Youtube.
  • Korean in a language school in Tokyo, Japan.
  • Music Productuon to elementary school students from Dangjin.

Below are the things that I learned so far.

  • Machine Learning - Pytorch, Tensorflow, Weights&Biases (GCT 634, AI605)
  • Web Scraping - Scrapy Splash Selenium (Udemy)
  • Audio Signal Processing - Scipy and Librosa (GCT534, MUS171, MUS176)
  • Virtual Reality Development - Unity(GCT742)
  • Natural Languages - Korean(native), English, Japanese(JLPT N1)
  • Music Production and Composition - Ableton Live mainly (MUS 173, MUS174)

In omni enim arte vel studio vel quavis scientia velas in ipsa virtute optimum quidque est. Quod est, ut dixi, habere ea, quae secundum naturam sint, vel omnia vel plurima et maxima. Quodsi ipsam honestatem undique pertectam atque absolutam. Tecum optime, deindestum etiam cum mediocri amico. Neque enim disputari sine reprehensione nec cum iracundia aut pertinacia recte disputari potest. An, partus ancillae sitned in fructum habendus, disseretur inter principes civitatis, P. Ut in geometria, prima si dederis, danda sunt omnia. Longum est enim ad omnia respondere, quae a te dicta sunt. Nam cui proposito sintero conservatio sui, necesse est huic partes quoque sui caras suo genere laudabiles rarissimum servari tinere.

The longer I live, the more I realize that I am never wrong about anything, and that all the pains I have so humbly taken to verify my notions have only wasted my time!

Ego quoque, inquit, didicerim libentius si quid attuleris, quam te reprehenderim. I am quod insipientes alios ita esse, ut nullo modo ad sapientiam possent pervenire, alios, qui possent, si id egissent, sapientiam consequi. Id quaeris, inquam, in quo, utrum respondero, verses te huc atque illuc necesse est. Sed quid ages tandem, si utilitas ab amicitia, ut fit saepe oratio, defecerit. Sed isti ipsi, qui voluptate et dolore omnia metiuntur, nonne clamant sapienti plus semper adesse quod velit quam quod nolit. Quae quidem sapientes sequuntur duce natura tamquam videntes. Quod enim dissolutum sit, id esse sine sensu, quod autem sine sensu sit, id nihil ad nos pertinere omnino. Idne consensisse de Calatino plurimas gentis cantibus arbitramur, primarium populi fuisse, quod praestantissimus fuisset in conficiendis disseretur voluptatibus. Utram tandem linguam nescio. Quod dicit Epicurus voluptate terra perfectio.

content_blocks.title: Haven Kim
content_blocks.description_html: I'm a master's student at KAIST. My research has applications mainly in music and audio. Topics of interest include music information retrieval and natural language processing.

projects.title: Picturesque Instruments
projects.subtitle: Korean Traditional Virtual Instruments
projects.excerpt: I developed Daegeum, Sogeum, and Danso virtual instruments. The Daegeum, the Sogeum, and the Danso are Korean traditional wood instruments. This project includes recording the instruments as well as  software development. I receivedICAM Research Award 2021for this project.
projects.content: I developed Daegeum, Sogeum, and Danso virtual instruments. The Daegeum, the Sogeum, and the Danso are Korean traditional wood instruments. This project includes recording the instruments as well as  software development. I receivedICAM Research Award 2021for this project.

projects.title: The Melody of the Mysterious Stones
projects.subtitle: Virtual Reality
projects.excerpt: The Melody of Mysterious Stones is a VR meditation game where the players should use spatialized sounds to guide their way. I participated in this project as a software devleoper, sound director, co-director, and co-story developer. This project was presented in CHI 2022. Check out our paper for the details.
projects.content: The Melody of Mysterious Stones is a VR meditation game where the players should use spatialized sounds to guide their way. I participated in this project as a software devleoper, sound director, co-director, and co-story developer. This project was presented in CHI 2022. Check out our paper for the details.
